data.title: Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech Signs MoU with Blockchain Firm
data.description:
Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech entered into a memorandum of understanding with Conflux Hong Kong Management to cooperate on the infrastructure and common key technologies related to the Conflux blockchain, a Tuesday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The cooperation scope includes the research and development of innovative technologies, and exploration of application scenarios with possible equity cooperation.

Hong Kong-incorporated Conflux engages mainly in the development of blockchain technology.

data.title: BNP Paribas Exane Adjusts Price Target on GE Healthcare Technologies to $83 From $96, Maintains Outperform Rating
data.description: GE Healthcare Technologies has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $86.76, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Oil Pares Gains After Tuesday Rebound
data.description:
WTI crude oil hovered near $59 per barrel, trimming earlier losses after a 3.4% rebound the day before.

Investors are closely watching for official US stockpile data, with forecasts pointing to an 800,000-barrel decline, following a larger-than-expected 4.5 million-barrel drop reported by the API. Oil markets remain under pressure after OPEC+ announced plans to accelerate production increases, raising oversupply fears at a time when US tariffs have dampened the global demand outlook.

Despite the recent price slump to four-year lows, signs of a potential supply correction are emerging.

Some US producers, including Diamondback Energy and Coterra Energy, are responding by reducing drilling activity, which analysts believe could tighten supply and help lift prices over time.

Meanwhile, traders are also awaiting the Federal Reserve‚Äôs rate decision, with expectations that it will hold rates steady amid growing economic uncertainty linked to ongoing trade tensions.

data.title: Century Enka's Consolidated Net Profit Plummets in Fiscal Q4
data.description:
Century Enka said its net profit plunged to 67.6 million Indian rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter from 202.7 million rupees a year earlier, according to a Tuesday filing.

Earnings per share for the three months ended March 31 fell to 3.09 rupees from 9.28 rupees a year ago.

Revenue from operations of the nylon and polyester filament yarns manufacturer increased year on year to 4.44 billion rupees from 4.69 billion rupees.

The board of Century Enka declared a dividend of 10 rupees per share for the fiscal year ended March 31.

data.title: Weis Markets' Fiscal Q1 Net Income Drops, Revenue Rises
data.description:
Weis Markets reported fiscal Q1 net income Wednesday of $0.76 per diluted share, down from $0.86 a year earlier.

Revenue for the quarter ended March 29 was $1.2 billion, up from $1.18 billion a year earlier.

Analyst estimates were not available for comparison.

data.title: Brent Crude Oil Hovers at $62
data.description:
Brent crude oil hovered near $62 per barrel, trimming earlier losses after a 3.4% rebound the day before.

Investors are closely watching for official US stockpile data, with forecasts pointing to an 800,000-barrel decline, following a larger-than-expected 4.5 million-barrel drop reported by the API. Oil markets remain under pressure after OPEC+ announced plans to accelerate production increases, raising oversupply fears at a time when US tariffs have dampened the global demand outlook.

Despite the recent price slump to four-year lows, signs of a potential supply correction are emerging.

Some US producers, including Diamondback Energy and Coterra Energy, are responding by reducing drilling activity, which analysts believe could tighten supply and help lift prices over time.

Meanwhile, traders are also awaiting the Federal Reserve‚Äôs rate decision, with expectations that it will hold rates steady amid growing economic uncertainty linked to ongoing trade tensions.

data.title: UK Regulator Seeks Feedback on Omnicom-Interpublic Group Deal
data.description:
The UK's Competition and Markets Authority invited interested parties to comment on the potential impact of Omnicom Group's planned takeover of The Interpublic Group of Companies on competition within the British markets.

The regulator said Wednesday that the invitation is part of its initial information-gathering process, adding that it has not launched a formal investigation into the advertising companies' transaction.

The deadline for submitting feedback is May 21.

data.title: Swiss 10-Year Government Bond Yield at Over 3-Year Low
data.description:
The yield on the 10-year Swiss government bond hovered around 0.10%, its lowest since January 2022, as investors priced in expectations for lower rates.

The Swiss headline inflation rate dropped to 0% year-on-year in April, bringing it just one step away from deflation, while the core inflation rate also saw a surprisingly sharp decline.

This supported bets of another rate cut on June 19.

In the meantime, Chair Martin Schlegel indicated that the Swiss National Bank (SNB) stands ready to reduce its key interest rate even further in light of economic uncertainty, including the potential for reintroducing a zero or negative interest rate policy.

data.title: Kennametal's Fiscal Q3 Adjusted Earnings Rise, Sales Fall
data.description:
Kennametal reported fiscal Q3 adjusted earnings Wednesday of $0.47 per diluted share, up from $0.30 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.24.

Sales for the quarter ended March 31 were $486.4 million, down from $515.8 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $489.3 million.

The company said it now expects fiscal 2025 adjusted EPS of $1.30 to $1.45, compared with its prior guidance of between $1.05 and $1.30. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $1.13.

Sales for the fiscal year are now expected to be $1.97 billion to $1.99 billion, compared with previous expectations of between $1.95 billion and $2 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect $1.97 billion.

Shares of the company were up 7% in recent Wednesday premarket activity.

data.title: Romania's Telecredit IFN 2024 revenue soars 76%
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), May 7 (SeeNews) - Romanian non-bank lender Telecredit IFN, operating under the Omnicredit brand, said on Wednesday that it has recorded revenues of 3.5 million euro ($3.97 million) in 2024, surging 76% year-on-year, as well as a net profit of 1.3 million euro.

The value of transactions financed by Telecredit IFN in 2024 increased by an annual 48% last year to 60.8 million euro, the non-bank lender said in a press release.

‚ÄúWe expect to maintain year-on-year growth in the value of financing at around 50% in 2025," Telecredit IFN CEO Elisa Rusu said.

Last year, Poland-based non-bank lender PragmaGO entered the Romanian market by acquiring 89% of Telecredit IFN for 5.785 million euro plus an earn-out of up to 0.445 million euro.

Telecredit IFN focuses on providing non-bank financing solutions to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) through factoring and digital lending.

($ = 0.8805 euro)

data.title: Tadawul Close Lower Amid Geopolitical Tensions, Fed Jitters
data.description:
The Tadawul All Share Index is back in the red by 0.31% on Wednesday's close, as conflict between Pakistan and India, as well as tariff talks, weighed on investor sentiment.

Aside from the US Fed decision that is only a few hours away from being announced, a major drag on sentiment was India's missile strikes on Pakistan. Worries are growing about potential retaliation for the fatal attack, Reuters reported.

In other news, the US and China are planning to hold trade negotiations in Geneva, Switzerland, regarding tariffs on Saturday. Reuters reported that the talks could help resolve the ongoing trade war between the two nations.

Leejam Sports Co. (SASE:1830), Lumi Rental Co. (SASE:4262), and Riyadh Cables Group (SASE:4142) are among the Saudi-listed companies that reported their latest financial results for the March quarter. Saudi Arabian Mining (SASE:1211), d/b/a Ma'aden, gained 0.59% at closing after it reported a 57.886% climb in net profit for the three months ended March 31, while net profit increased 15.822%.

"We are off to a great start in 2025. We are building on the momentum of last year and continuing our progress across all operations, with strong production results, safety improvements, exploration success, project advancement and portfolio consolidation," Ma'aden Chief Executive Officer Bob Wilt commented.

Leejam Sports lost the most out of constituents at 10%, while Lumi Rental and Riyadh Cables closed 0.65% and 0.95% higher, respectively.

In other news, Saudi Top for Trading (SASE:9552) shares closed flat as it secured a five-year Sharia-compliant funding worth 21 million Saudi riyals from Bank Aljazira.

data.title: ELLWEE AB (publ) receives observation status
data.description:
Nasdaq Stockholm ABFirst North Growth Market - Equities

ELLWEE AB (publ) receives observation status

On May 6,¬Ý2025, ELLWEE AB (publ) (the ‚ÄúCompany‚Äù) issued a press release withinformation that it has entered into a letter of intent for a reverse takeoverof Ortelius International AB, conditional upon, approval by a general meetingof the Company's shareholders.

On May 7,¬Ý2025, the Company disclosed its interim report for the first quarter2025 with information on the Company's financial situation.

According to item 2.5.1 of the Nasdaq First North Growth Market Rulebook, theExchange may for an issuer that undergoes substantial changes initiate arenewed review process of the issuer's fulfilment of applicable admissionrequirements. With reference to the planned reversed takeover, the Exchange hasdecided that the Company shall undergo such a review process, after which theExchange will decide whether to admit the Company¬¥s shares for continuedtrading on Nasdaq First North Growth Market.

The rules of Nasdaq First North Growth Market also state that an issuer may begiven observation status if it has been the subject of a reverse takeover orotherwise plans to make or has been subject to an extensive change in itsbusiness or organization so that the issuer upon an overall assessment appearsto be an entirely new company.

Furthermore, the rules of Nasdaq First North Growth Market state that an issuercan be given observation status if there is material adverse uncertainty inrespect of the issuer's financial position.

With reference to the above, Nasdaq Stockholm AB decides that the shares inELLWEE AB (publ) (ELLWEE, ISIN code SE0020551679, order book ID 212605) shallbe given observation status.

For further information concerning this exchange notice please contactEnforcement & Investigations, telephone + 46¬Ý8¬Ý405¬Ý70¬Ý50.https://view.news.eu.nasdaq.com/view?id=b59afc82217e349d847d2a3a5f15775b7&lang=en&src=omxlink

data.title: Oil Trades Higher on Hopes for End to the China-U.S. Trade War and a Drop in U.S. Inventories
data.description:
Oil traded higher for a second day early on Wednesday, rebounding from a four-year low touched Monday as China and the United States agreed to stage trade talks while a report showed U.S. inventories fell last week.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil for June delivery was last seen up US$0.42 to US$59.51 per barrel, while July Brent crude was up US$0.39 to US$62.54.

Prices on Monday fell to the lowest since February 2021 after OPEC+ said it plans to add a second monthly supply increase of 411,000 barrels per day in June as it looks to speed the return of 2.2-million bpd of voluntary production cuts and punish cartel members producing above their quotas.

"The move, largely driven by Saudi Arabia, appears to be a calculated response to persistent non-compliance within the group-particularly from Kazakhstan-and also aligns with former President Trump's push for lower oil prices. Over time, OPEC could benefit from this strategy through increased market share, as sustained low prices may challenge the economic viability of higher-cost producers," Ole Hansen, head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank, wrote.

With the market pricing in the supply hike by cutting oil prices 12% from the month high of US$64.68 on April 17 to Monday's low of US$57.13, traders were ready to bid oil on good news. That came as the United States and China said they plan to stage talks to end their trade war following U.S. President Donald Trump's imposition of 145% tariffs on imports from China last month, while China retaliated with punishing tariffs on U.S. goods, nearly ending all trade between the world's two largest economies.

"Markets this morning, including oil, are cock-a-hoop that the US and China are to finally meet in Geneva this weekend and after a relief rally yesterday by oil from year-to-date lows, expectancy of ice-breaking talks takes up the baton of hope for the moment," PVM Oil Associates noted.

A sharp drop in U.S. oil inventories last week also supported oil prices. In its weekly survey, the American Petroleum Institute reported inventories fell by 4.49-million barrels last week, while analysts polled by Oilprice.com expected a draw of 2.5-million barrels on average. The Energy Information Administration will release official inventory data later on Wednesday morning.

data.title: As investors await first Fed decision since 'liberation day' tariffs, China is a 'fulcrum' in markets
data.description:
By Christine Idzelis

Tariffs may leave a 'significant divot in growth,' says BlackRock's Rick Rieder

The Federal Reserve will conclude on Wednesday its first policy meeting since President Donald Trump rocked markets with his "liberation day" tariffs announcement on April 2, as investors watch for their ripple effect on the economy and the Fed's interest rate path.

Tariffs may leave a "significant divot in growth," as chief executives at companies "sit on their hands and wait" amid uncertainty about where the levies will ultimately settle, said Rick Rieder, BlackRock's chief investment officer of global fixed income and head of the firm's global allocation investment team. He added that consumers risk pulling back on spending for a bit after some "sticker shock."

"We're going to see a slowdown in the economy," which could be "short-lived," he told MarketWatch in a phone interview. But with inflation running high and the labor market still holding up, it's "pretty hard" to see the Fed cutting interest rates before July, he said.

In the meantime, the market anticipates the U.S. may get some trade deals done before the 90-day pause that Trump authorized on his April 2 tariffs expires in July.

"The one that I think will be the fulcrum on the market is China," said Rieder. "That is the big one," he said, as the "interplay with the U.S. economy is so intense."

That means every piece of news relating to the trade discussions - or lack thereof - between the U.S. and China may be market-moving, across stocks, rates, gold and currencies, according to Rieder. "All eyes in the market are focused on China," partly because the current tariffs are so large and "there are so many deliveries that come from China that are not being made today."

U.S. stocks had tumbled after Trump announced a sweeping set of so-called reciprocal tariffs on April 2, but the S&P 500 SPX rebounded after he hit pause on those levies outside China.

Investors are watching for trade deals that may lower the size of those tariffs.

"On tariffs, it's still hard to see the administration wrapping up multiple bilateral trade deals by July 9th, when the 90-day pause on so-called reciprocal tariffs is supposed to end," David Kelly, chief global strategist at J.P. Morgan Asset Management, said in a May 5 note. "We expect that, for most countries, the pause will be extended, while leaving a 10% universal tariff in place.

Trade tensions between the U.S. and China quickly escalated after April 2, with tit-for-tat tariffs skyrocketing to unsustainable levels. Trump raised tariffs on imports from China to 145%.

The Treasury Department announced on Tuesday that Secretary Scott Bessent will travel on May 8 to Switzerland, where he will meet with China's "lead representative on economic matters." The statement did not provide further details on the planned meeting.

"The Chinese tariff issue is more problematic since, although the Chinese economy is clearly suffering from the impact of these tariffs, the Chinese government may want to play hardball, figuring that the pressure on their government from unemployed manufacturing workers will be easier to absorb than the pressure on the U.S. administration from a lack of Chinese goods for consumers and businesses," said Kelly. "That being said, some eventual compromise, at much lower tariffs than their current triple-digit levels, is likely."

Fed Chair Jerome Powell has recently indicated that the central bank is assessing the impact of still uncertain tariffs on the economy, saying in an April 16 speech in Chicago that the effects would likely include higher inflation, at least temporarily, and slower growth based on what had so far been announced by the White House.

Inflation is already elevated above the Fed's 2% target. The three-month moving average of core inflation, as measured by the personal-consumption expenditures price index, is running at more than 3.5%, said Rieder. Core PCE, which excludes food and energy prices, is the Fed's favored gauge of inflation.

Powell will host a press conference on monetary policy on Wednesday at 2:30 p.m. Eastern, shortly after the Federal Open Market Committee concludes its two-day policy meeting.

The FOMC will release its decision on rates at 2 p.m. Eastern on Wednesday.

Investors expect the Fed to keep its benchmark rate steady at the current range of 4.25% to 4.5%.

Should the labor market deteriorate in the coming months, the Fed could move quickly with a large rate cut of 50 basis points, said Rieder. But the central bank also has other tools to address any "stresses" in the financial system, he said, including using its balance sheet.

The Fed's decision at its March policy meeting to reduce quantitative tightening, or slow the shrinking of its balance sheet, helped increase "liquidity" in the market, according to Rieder.

In the bond market, the yield on the 10-year Treasury note BX:TMUBMUSD10Y was edging up about one basis point on Wednesday morning to around 4.31%. The 2-year Treasury rate BX:TMUBMUSD02Y was up about 2 basis points to around 3.81%.

As for equities, futures contracts in the U.S. stock market were pointing to potential gains at the opening bell for the S&P 500, Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA and technology-heavy Nasdaq Composite COMP, according to FactSet data, at last check. So far in May, all three major benchmarks were in positive territory through Tuesday, with the S&P 500 up 0.7% month to date after dropping in April.

-Christine Idzelis

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Aligned and Lambda Partner to Power Next-Generation AI Infrastructure
data.description:
DALLAS, May 07,¬Ý2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) ‚Äî Aligned Data Centers, a leading technology infrastructure company offering innovative, sustainable and adaptive Scale Data Centers and Build-to-Scale solutions for global hyperscale and enterprise customers, today announced a partnership with Lambda, the AI Developer Cloud, to provide customers with data center infrastructure and an AI cloud platform ready to sustainably accelerate their AI growth. Lambda will occupy Aligned‚Äôs newest Dallas-Fort Worth area facility, DFW-04, which will be designed to be a liquid-cooled data center capable of supporting the highest-density Graphics Processing Units (GPUs).

‚ÄúWe‚Äôre proud to partner with Lambda to support the buildout of its GPU cloud infrastructure, accelerated by NVIDIA, for AI deployments, which is transforming how AI developers innovate and businesses utilizing AI models operate,‚Äù states Andrew Schaap, CEO of Aligned. ‚ÄúParticularly in Dallas, where demand for AI computation space has spiked interest in a growing market, combining a GPU cloud¬Ýbuilt specifically for AI workloads with an AI-ready data center designed with liquid cooling technologies capable of supporting the highest-density environments will be a game changer.‚Äù

The company‚Äôs ultra-scalable and flexible Adaptive Modular Infrastructure (AMI) and innovative air, liquid, and hybrid cooling solutions future-proof IT deployments, mitigate IT obsolescence, and maximize asset lifespan.

While committing to sustainability as a core pillar of its customer-focused business strategy, the company has been an industry leader in advancing data center energy efficiency and cooling technologies for more than a decade, including its patented and award-winning Delta3‚Ñ¢ air-cooled system. Aligned's patent-pending DeltaFlow‚Ñ¢ liquid cooling system delivers unparalleled performance for AI innovation, supporting virtually any density and GPU cloud requirement. The company‚Äôs scalable, sustainable infrastructure is enabling next-generation AI workloads. Aligned's partnership with Lambda exemplifies its dedication to leading AI service providers.

Lambda's AI Developer Cloud is trusted by world-renowned¬ÝAI engineers and industry pioneers who have shaped modern artificial intelligence. The organization‚Äôs customers include some of the most successful and best-known technology companies, trillion-dollar capitalization enterprises, academic and research institutions in the world, as well as AI startups. The partnership with Aligned will see Lambda‚Äôs AI Cloud platform integrated into the company‚Äôs new DFW-04 data center, which is currently under construction in Plano, Texas. A determinative factor of this alliance is that Aligned‚Äôs seamless and adaptive infrastructure for next-gen AI will easily support infrastructure accelerated by NVIDIA Blackwell and NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra, as well as the future generations of AI platforms.

‚ÄúWith its unrelenting focus on driving disruptive innovation in data center design, energy efficiency and cooling, Aligned is the ideal partner to help Lambda build large, flexible space that meets the AI demands of today and tomorrow,‚Äù comments Lambda VP, Data Center Infrastructure, Ken Patchett. ‚ÄúDeploying AI at scale is no easy feat, and Aligned‚Äôs ability to rapidly deliver AI-ready infrastructure, along with its passion for supporting customers with a consistently high-touch, world-class experience, is instrumental to meet the aggressive scale, quality and speed standards Lambda sets for its public and private deployments.‚Äù¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý ¬Ý¬Ý

About Aligned Data Centers

Aligned Data Centers is a leading technology infrastructure company offering innovative, sustainable, and adaptive Scale Data Centers and Build-to-Scale solutions for global hyperscale and enterprise customers. Our intelligent infrastructure allows densification and vertical growth within the same footprint, enabling customers to scale up without disruption, all while maintaining industry-leading Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE). By reducing the energy, water and space needed to operate, our data center solutions, combined with our patented cooling technology, offer businesses a competitive advantage by improving sustainability, reliability, and their bottom line. About Lambda

Lambda was founded in 2012 by AI engineers with published research at the top machine learning conferences in the world. Our GPU cloud and on-prem hardware enables AI developers to easily, securely and affordably build, test and deploy AI products at scale. Lambda‚Äôs mission is to accelerate human progress with ubiquitous and affordable access to computation. One person, one GPU.

data.title: A US producer offers High density polyethylene (HDPE Film) in Southeast Asia
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Southeast Asia stated that a producer from the US is heard to have announced its offer for HDPE Film grades at the USD 920/mt levels.This offer is for May 2025 shipment on CFR SEA Main port basis.

data.title: A South Asian producer offers Linear low density polyethylene (LLDPE Film) in Nepal
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Nepal reported that a producer from South Asia is heard to have offered its LLDPE Film grade at the USD 1035/mt levels.This offer is for May 2025 delivery on Ex Works basis.
